name: Mark Andrews
headline: Andrews' Efficiency Cratered, But The Volume Lever Is There
body:
The 2025 tape was a problem. Andrews dropped to TE27 on 1.27 yards per route run, a fall from his TE9 mark a year earlier when he posted 1.96. His EPA per play collapsed from 0.726 to a flat 0.022, and his 50% success rate told the same story as the 8.79 yards per reception. He still drew 70 targets and a 17.2% share across all 17 games, but the production thinned to 48 grabs, 422 yards, and five scores.

The path back is real. He held 15 red-zone looks and nine end-zone targets, so the touchdown equity that fueled his 11-score 2024 hasn't vanished. With Isaiah Likely gone to New York, Andrews is the only proven seam threat in Baltimore, and a healthier Lamar Jackson lifts the floor. He's a late-round dart with mid-range TE1 ceiling if the offense normalizes.
